title: guapeba laevigata
intro: Guapeba laevigata is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, known scientifically as Guarea macrophylla, which belongs to the family Meliaceae. Guapeba laevigata has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used to treat various health conditions, offering health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Guapeba laevigata can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it a valuable resource in traditional herbal medicine.
